author: gbolahan
content:
Hello, I've open-sourced eth-state-diff, a library focused specifically on disk-level archival of beacon states.

It uses domain-specific encoders to compress 32 epochs of mainnet state down to ~900KB (zstd lvl 3)

**The Performance Profile:**
The library itself is highly optimized for memory bandwidth:
• create on flat arrays: ~85ms (mostly AVX2 memcmp on validators)
• apply on flat arrays: ~18ms
• Serialized + compressed: ~21ms

**The Integration Reality (Important Context):**
Because achieving that 85ms requires linear SIMD scans, the API takes flat primitive vectors (&[u64], &[u8], &[[u8; 32]]). 

The biggest overhead is in collecting the trees to be passed as inputs for the library. A naive approach with Grandine took about 250ms

**Open Work:**
Right now, clients bridge the gap by serializing/collecting their trees into flat vectors. The biggest area for future optimization is improving this boundary

Repo: https://github.com/galadd/eth_state_diff
Crates.io: https://crates.io/crates/eth-state-diff


Let me know what you'd like to see
